title: Treat a Minor Burn
summary: Cool and protect a minor burn from a hot pan, curling iron, or other everyday heat source to reduce pain and help the skin heal.
learnerPromise: Cool and protect a minor burn to reduce pain and help healing.
whyItMatters: Minor burns are common at home and can be painful. Treating them quickly helps the skin heal and lowers the chance of scarring or infection.
realLifeUses: Touching a hot pan while cooking
Brushing against a hot hair styling tool
Accidentally touching a hot mug of coffee or tea
youWillLearn: How to cool a burn with running water
How to cover the burn with a clean, loose covering
What not to put on a burn
When a burn needs medical attention
miniChallenge: Learn the cool-cover-check routine and explain it to someone else.
steps: Cool the burn under cool running water for several minutes as soon as possible.
Remove rings, watches, or tight items near the area before it swells.
Pat the area dry gently with a clean cloth.
Cover the burn with a clean, loose bandage or sterile non-stick dressing.
Check the area daily for signs of infection like growing redness or swelling.
completionCriteria: You cooled the burn under running water right away
You covered it with a clean, loose dressing
You know to watch for signs that need medical care
commonProblems: The burn area is large or on the face — seek medical help
Blisters form — do not pop them; cover and protect them
The pain gets worse over time — this may need a doctor's care
tips: Do not use ice, butter, or toothpaste on a burn — these can make it worse
Cool the burn for at least 10 to 20 minutes under running water
Keep a few sterile non-stick dressings in a first aid kit
helpBoundary: Seek medical help for burns larger than your palm, on the face, or caused by chemicals or electricity.
